evals.prompt: Commit my staged changes. I've added the M3LError hierarchy and M3LResult type in packages/m3l-common/src/core/errors/. These surface through the ./core barrel; the three-entry exports map is unchanged.
evals.expected_output: A git commit with: subject starting with 'feat:' in lowercase imperative tense ≤70 chars; body mentioning the public symbols (M3LError, M3LResult); a semver impact statement noting the exports map is unchanged; a Co-Authored-By footer.

assertions.text: Subject line starts with 'feat:' in lowercase
assertions.check: The commit subject must begin with exactly 'feat:' (lowercase, no capitalization)

assertions.text: Subject line uses imperative present tense (e.g. 'implement' or 'add', not 'implemented' or 'added')
assertions.check: The verb in the subject must be imperative present tense

assertions.text: Subject line is 70 characters or fewer
assertions.check: Count the characters in the subject line — must be ≤70

assertions.text: Body mentions at least one public symbol (M3LError, M3LResult, M3LErrorUtils, or similar)
assertions.check: The commit body names at least one of the public symbols being introduced

assertions.text: Body includes a semver impact statement (e.g. 'exports map unchanged' or 'no semver break')
assertions.check: The body ends with or includes a statement about semver impact

assertions.text: Body includes a Co-Authored-By footer with noreply@anthropic.com
assertions.check: Footer line matches 'Co-Authored-By: Claude ... <noreply@anthropic.com>'

evals.prompt: Commit the staged change — I just ran prettier on pnpm-workspace.yaml, nothing else changed.
evals.expected_output: A subject-only commit: 'chore: prettier format workspace file' or similar — no body, since this is a mechanical formatting run.

assertions.text: Subject line starts with 'chore:' in lowercase
assertions.check: The commit subject must begin with exactly 'chore:' (lowercase)

assertions.text: Subject line is 70 characters or fewer
assertions.check: Count the characters in the subject line — must be ≤70

assertions.text: No body is generated (mechanical formatting change needs no explanation)
assertions.check: The commit message should have no body — just the subject line

evals.prompt: Commit this — I edited .github/workflows/claude-pr-review.yml to add a condition that skips the workflow run when the PR author is dependabot[bot].
evals.expected_output: A subject-only commit: 'ci: do not review dependabot PRs' or similar — no scope, no body.

assertions.text: Subject line starts with 'ci:' in lowercase (no scope like 'ci(workflows):')
assertions.check: The commit subject must begin with 'ci:' — no scope parentheses

assertions.text: Subject line is 70 characters or fewer
assertions.check: Count the characters in the subject line — must be ≤70

assertions.text: No body generated (single-line CI tweak is self-explanatory)
assertions.check: The commit message should have no body

evals.prompt: Commit my staged changes. I renamed M3LPaths.outputDir to M3LPaths.archiveDir everywhere — internal call-sites and the public type. This is a breaking change for any consumer using the old property name.
evals.expected_output: A feat! commit with: 'feat!:' subject naming the renamed symbol; body explaining the reason + which call-sites changed; migration instructions; BREAKING CHANGE footer; Co-Authored-By footer.

assertions.text: Subject line starts with 'feat!:' (breaking change marker present)
assertions.check: The commit subject must begin with exactly 'feat!:'

assertions.text: Subject line names the renamed symbol (outputDir or archiveDir)
assertions.check: The subject mentions at least one of the two property names

assertions.text: Body includes migration instructions (what consumers must change)
assertions.check: The body must tell consumers how to update their code

assertions.text: Body includes a BREAKING CHANGE note (footer or prose)
assertions.check: The body ends with or contains 'BREAKING CHANGE:' naming what changed

assertions.text: Body includes a Co-Authored-By footer with noreply@anthropic.com
assertions.check: Footer line matches 'Co-Authored-By: Claude ... <noreply@anthropic.com>'

evals.prompt: Commit this. I updated the post-edit-verify hook so it now runs eslint in the loop (after prettier, before typecheck). I also edited the implement-submodule spoke prompt to front-load contract nuances. No src/, test, or exports-map changes.
evals.expected_output: A chore commit with a body: subject names what changed; body explains why (what problem this solves); semver impact statement; Co-Authored-By footer.

assertions.text: Subject line starts with 'chore:' in lowercase
assertions.check: The commit subject must begin with exactly 'chore:'

assertions.text: Subject line is 70 characters or fewer
assertions.check: Count the characters in the subject line — must be ≤70

assertions.text: A body is present (this chore has a non-obvious why)
assertions.check: The commit message must have a body below the subject

assertions.text: Body includes a semver impact statement ('no semver impact', 'zero semver impact', or equivalent)
assertions.check: The body contains a statement confirming no release is triggered

assertions.text: Body includes a Co-Authored-By footer with noreply@anthropic.com
assertions.check: Footer line matches 'Co-Authored-By: Claude ... <noreply@anthropic.com>'
